nodes.statement: A diffusion model trained with differentiable stylized-fact losses reduces tail-index error in generated financial returns compared with the same diffusion model without those losses.
nodes.rationale: Tail fidelity is central to risk-relevant financial scenario generation.

nodes.statement: Conditioning a financial diffusion model on target volatility produces generated return paths whose realized volatility is monotonic in the requested volatility level.
nodes.rationale: Faithful volatility control is a core requirement for auditable stress scenarios.

nodes.statement: Signature-based path metrics identify temporal-dependence failures in generated financial time series that are not detected by marginal return-distribution metrics.
nodes.rationale: The idea depends on evaluating path-level fidelity beyond pointwise realism.
